# Chapter 1 — The Cliff's Edge

The wind howled across the cliff's edge, tugging at Elara's cloak as she stared down into the mist-shrouded valley below. She had been climbing for the better part of three hours, her fingers numb and her lungs burning, and now the summit refused to reveal itself.

"Just a little further," she whispered to no one in particular.

Behind her, Kael stumbled over a loose stone and swore under his breath. He had argued against this path since dawn, insisting the eastern trail was safer, but Elara had overridden him. The eastern trail was a day's detour, and they did not have a day to spare. The Ashen King's riders would be on the road by nightfall.

"Do you ever admit when you're wrong?" Kael asked, catching up to her. His dark hair was plastered to his forehead with sweat, and there was a fresh scrape along his jaw.

"I'm not wrong," Elara said flatly. "I'm just early."

He laughed despite himself — that short, surprised sound she had come to recognize over six months of forced partnership. Six months ago she would have slit his throat for the bounty on his head. Now she trusted him at her back, which was either growth or madness, and she could no longer tell the difference.

The mist parted suddenly, as if cut by a blade, and the citadel emerged: black stone towers clawing at a bruised sky, bridges of light arcing between them, and at the highest peak a single window glowing red. The Heartfire. After everything — the burned villages, the dead, the long road north — it was real, and it was here.

"We're here," Kael breathed.

Elara did not answer. Her hand had found the hilt of the dagger at her belt, the one her mother had given her the night the soldiers came. The night everything changed. She drew a slow breath, tasting ash on the wind.

"We go in," she said. "We finish this."

And she stepped forward into the dark.
